qa_pairs.question: Which software stack will Trillium use?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium will utilize the CVMFS software stack, which is also available on other national clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of the NiaEnv software stack on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The default NiaEnv software stack, previously available on Niagara, will be retired and will not be available on Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: Will the MistEnv software stack be available on Trillium-gpu?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the MistEnv software stack from Mist will not be available on Trillium-gpu.

qa_pairs.question: Which modules are loaded by default on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: On Trillium, only the `gentoo/2023` and `CCconfig` modules will be loaded by default.

qa_pairs.question: Do I need to explicitly load all software modules on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, `gentoo/2023` and `CCconfig` modules are loaded by default. However, all other modules for compilers, libraries, Python, and similar tools will need to be explicitly loaded.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of modules require explicit loading on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Modules for compilers, libraries, Python, and similar tools will have to be explicitly loaded on Trillium.
